attributes.description: Fires when the 5-minute load average, divided by the reported logical CPU count, stays above 1.0. That matches `normalized load` > 1.0. Tune the comparator in ESQL (e.g. 1.2) to reduce noise on bursty hosts.
attributes.name: [System Host Metrics OTel] Load average sustained above CPU count

investigation_guide.blob:
## Normalised load > 1.0

1. Check CPU utilisation by state; high I/O wait often correlates with load (see disk panels).
2. Review `system.disk.pending_operations` and disk I/O on the same host.
3. If load is high but CPU util is low, suspect I/O or uninterruptible sleep.

**Tune** Edit `1.0` in `norm_load > 1.0` (e.g. 1.2) for noisier hosts.

esqlQuery.esql:
FROM metrics-hostmetricsreceiver.otel-*
| STATS load5 = AVG(`metrics.system.cpu.load_average.5m`), ncpu = MAX(`metrics.system.cpu.logical.count`) BY `host.name`
| WHERE load5 IS NOT NULL AND ncpu IS NOT NULL AND ncpu > 0
| EVAL norm_load = load5 / to_double(ncpu)
| WHERE norm_load > 1.0
| SORT norm_load DESC
| LIMIT 20
